<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 数据模型定义
// ============================================

class TestObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Create observer relationship and test notification
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return std::pair<bool, int> Pair of whether observer was notified and call count
 */
std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Reset thread manager for clean test state
    reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance().resetForTesting();
    
    // Create observer and target nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Add observer relationship (observer observes target)
    observerGraph.addObserver(observer, target);
    
    // Notify target of change
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Return notification status and call count
    return {observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount()};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test atomic operations on reactive variables
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value for the reactive variable
 * @param addValue Value to add atomically
 * @return int The final value after atomic addition
 */
int test_atomic_operations(int initialValue, int addValue) {
    // Create a reactive variable using reaction::var with the initial value
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Use the += operator to perform atomic addition
    reactiveVar += addValue;
    
    // Use the get() method to retrieve the current value
    // and return the result
    return reactiveVar.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test observer node naming functionality
 *
 * @param name Name to set for the observer node
 * @return std::string The retrieved name from the observer graph
 */
std::string test_observer_naming(const std::string& name) {
    // 1. Create observer node
    reaction::ObserverNode node;
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Set node name using setName method
    graph.setName(&node, name);
    
    // 4. Retrieve node name using getName method
    return graph.getName(&node);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestObserverReset : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverReset(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false), m_callCount(0) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test observer reset functionality
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return bool Whether observer reset worked correctly (observer no longer notified after reset)
 */
bool test_observer_reset(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // 1. Create observer nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(targetName);
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Add observer relationship
    observerGraph.addObserver(target, observer);
    
    // 4. Notify target and verify observer was notified
    target->valueChanged(true);
    if (!observer->wasChanged()) {
        return false; // Observer wasn't notified initially
    }
    
    // 5. Record call count before reset
    int callCountBeforeReset = observer->getCallCount();
    
    // 6. Reset observer node
    observer->reset();
    
    // 7. Notify target again
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // 8. Verify observer was not notified after reset
    bool observerNotNotified = !observer->wasChanged();
    bool callCountUnchanged = observer->getCallCount() == callCountBeforeReset;
    
    // 9. Return success status
    return observerNotNotified && callCountUnchanged;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

    TestObserverClosure(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestMultipleObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestMultipleObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test multiple observers watching a single target
 *
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @param observerNames Vector of names for observer nodes
 * @return std::pair<int, bool> Pair of total call count and whether all observers were notified
 */
std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    // 1. Create target node
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);
    
    // 2. Create observer nodes
    std::vector<std::shared_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        observers.push_back(std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(name));
    }
    
    // 3. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 4. Add all observers to watch target
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        graph.addObserver(target, observer);
    }
    
    // 5. Notify target
    target->valueChanged();
    
    // 6. Count notifications and verify all observers were notified
    int totalCallCount = 0;
    bool allNotified = true;
    
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        totalCallCount += observer->getCallCount();
        if (observer->getCallCount() == 0 || !observer->wasChanged()) {
            allNotified = false;
        }
    }
    
    // 7. Return total call count and notification status
    return {totalCallCount, allNotified};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test resetting a calculation node with new dependencies
 *
 * @param a First input value
 * @param b Second input value
 * @param c Third input value
 * @return int The result after resetting calculation to use different dependencies
 */
int test_calculation_reset(int a, int b, int c) {
    // Create reactive variables from input values
    auto var_a = reaction::var(a);
    auto var_b = reaction::var(b);
    auto var_c = reaction::var(c);
    
    // Create initial calculation that sums a and b
    auto calc_node = reaction::calc([](int x, int y) { return x + y; }, var_a, var_b);
    
    // Reset the calculation to use different dependencies (b and c)
    calc_node.reset([](int x, int y) { return x + y; }, var_b, var_c);
    
    // Return the result after reset
    return calc_node.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class PersonRecord : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age);

    const std::string& getName() const;
    int getAge() const;

    void setName(const std::string& name);
    void setAge(int age);

private:
    reaction::Var<std::string> m_name;
    reaction::Var<int> m_age;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test reactive fields updating calculated info
 *
 * @param initialName Initial person name
 * @param initialAge Initial person age
 * @param newName Updated person name
 * @param newAge Updated person age
 * @return std::string Concatenated info after updates
 */
std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    // 1. Create reactive PersonRecord variable
    auto person = reaction::var(PersonRecord(initialName, initialAge));
    
    // 2. Create calculation depending on reactive fields
    auto infoCalc = reaction::calc([](const PersonRecord& p) {
        return p.getName() + " " + std::to_string(p.getAge());
    }, person);
    
    // 3. Update name field
    person.get().setName(newName);
    
    // 4. Update age field
    person.get().setAge(newAge);
    
    // 5. Return updated info
    return infoCalc();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Explanation:**

1. **Reactive Variable Creation**: Used `reaction::var()` to create a reactive `PersonRecord` variable with initial values
2. **Reactive Calculation**: Created a calculation using `reaction::calc()` that depends on the reactive `PersonRecord` fields and returns a formatted string
3. **Field Updates**: Used the `setName()` and `setAge()` methods to update the reactive fields
4. **Dependency Tracking**: The calculation automatically tracks dependencies on the reactive fields and updates when they change
5. **Result Retrieval**: Called the calculation to get the updated concatenated information

The implementation follows the reactive programming paradigm where changes to the source data (`PersonRecord` fields) automatically propagate to dependent calculations (`infoCalc`).</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test batch execution reduces recomputation count
 *
 * @param initialA Initial value for variable a
 * @param initialB Initial value for variable b
 * @param nonBatchA Value to set a without batching
 * @param nonBatchB Value to set b without batching
 * @param batchA Value to set a within batch
 * @param batchB Value to set b within batch
 * @return std::tuple<int, int, int> {final sum, count after non-batch updates, count after batch updates}
 */
std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                               int initialB,
                                               int nonBatchA,
                                               int nonBatchB,
                                               int batchA,
                                               int batchB) {
    // 1. Create reactive variables a and b with initial values
    auto a = reaction::var(initialA);
    auto b = reaction::var(initialB);
    
    // 2. Create variable to track recomputation count
    auto recompCount = reaction::var(0);
    
    // 3. Create calculation that sums a and b and tracks recomputation count
    auto sumCalc = reaction::calc([&]() {
        // Increment recompCount each time calculation runs
        recompCount.value(recompCount.get() + 1);
        return a.get() + b.get();
    });
    
    // Force initial computation to establish baseline
    int initialSum = sumCalc.get();
    
    // 4. Perform non-batch updates
    a.value(nonBatchA);
    b.value(nonBatchB);
    
    // 5. Record recompute count after non-batch updates
    int countAfterNonBatch = recompCount.get();
    
    // 6. Create batch context and execute batch
    auto batchContext = reaction::batch([&]() {
        a.value(batchA);
        b.value(batchB);
    });
    batchContext.execute();
    
    // 8. Record recompute count after batch updates
    int countAfterBatch = recompCount.get();
    
    // 9. Return final sum and counts
    return std::make_tuple(sumCalc.get(), countAfterNonBatch, countAfterBatch);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test naming reactive nodes and retrieving names
 *
 * @param valueA Initial value for varA
 * @param valueB Initial value for varB
 * @return std::string Combined string of retrieved names
 */
std::string test_named_reactions(int valueA, int valueB) {
    // Create reactive variables with names
    auto varA = reaction::var(valueA).setName("varA");
    auto varB = reaction::var(valueB).setName("varB");
    
    // Create calculations with names
    auto calc1 = reaction::calc([&]() { return varA() + varB(); }).setName("calc1");
    auto calc2 = reaction::calc([&]() { return varA() * varB(); }).setName("calc2");
    
    // Create action with name
    auto action1 = reaction::action([&]() { 
        return varA() - varB(); 
    }).setName("action1");
    
    // Retrieve names
    std::string nameA = varA.getName();
    std::string nameB = varB.getName();
    std::string nameCalc1 = calc1.getName();
    std::string nameCalc2 = calc2.getName();
    std::string nameAction1 = action1.getName();
    
    // Return combined names string
    return nameA + " " + nameB + " " + nameCalc1 + " " + nameCalc2 + " " + nameAction1;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ExceptionInfo {
    std::string formattedMessage;
    std::string originalMessage;
    std::string file;
    int line;
    std::string function;
    std::string codeName;
};

ExceptionInfo capture_exception_info(const std::string &message,
    reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode code,
    const std::string &file,
    int line,
    const std::string &function) {
    
    // Create ReactionException instance with provided parameters
    reaction::ReactionException exception(message, code, file, line, function);
    
    // Populate ExceptionInfo struct using exception's getter methods
    ExceptionInfo info;
    info.formattedMessage = exception.getFormattedMessage();
    info.originalMessage = exception.getOriginalMessage();
    info.file = exception.getFile();
    info.line = exception.getLine();
    info.function = exception.getFunction();
    info.codeName = reaction::ReactionException::errorCodeToString(exception.getErrorCode());
    
    return info;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ValiditySnapshot {
    bool firstValidBeforeClose;
    bool firstValidAfterClose;
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose;
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator bool and operator== for validity tracking.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the reactive variable
 * @return ValiditySnapshot Snapshot describing validity/equality before and after close
 */
inline ValiditySnapshot test_react_validity(int initialValue) {
    // 1. Create reactive variable
    auto source = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create copy of source
    auto copy = source;
    
    // 3. Check validity before close
    bool firstValidBeforeClose = static_cast<bool>(source);
    bool copyValidBeforeClose = static_cast<bool>(copy);
    
    // 4. Check equality before close
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose = (source == copy);
    
    // 5. Close source node
    source.close();
    
    // 6. Check validity after close
    bool firstValidAfterClose = static_cast<bool>(source);
    bool copyValidAfterClose = static_cast<bool>(copy);
    
    // 7. Check equality after close with exception handling
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose = false;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = false;
    
    try {
        copiesEqualAfterClose = (source == copy);
    } catch (...) {
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = true;
    }
    
    // 8. Return validity snapshot
    return ValiditySnapshot{
        firstValidBeforeClose,
        firstValidAfterClose,
        copiesEqualBeforeClose,
        copiesEqualAfterClose,
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <set>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

struct OrderingSnapshot {
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    bool allUnique;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator< for ordering reactive handles.
 *
 * @param firstName Name assigned to the first reactive node
 * @param secondName Name assigned to the second reactive node
 * @param thirdName Name assigned to the third reactive node
 * @return OrderingSnapshot Snapshot of sorted names and uniqueness flag
 */
inline OrderingSnapshot test_react_ordering(const std::string &firstName,
                                            const std::string &secondName,
                                            const std::string &thirdName) {
    // Create three reactive variables with the provided names
    auto var1 = reaction::var(0).setName(firstName);
    auto var2 = reaction::var(0).setName(secondName);
    auto var3 = reaction::var(0).setName(thirdName);
    
    // Insert them into a set (will order using React::operator<)
    std::set<decltype(var1)> reactSet;
    reactSet.insert(var1);
    reactSet.insert(var2);
    reactSet.insert(var3);
    
    // Collect ordered names
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    for (const auto& react : reactSet) {
        orderedNames.push_back(react.getName());
    }
    
    // Check if all names are unique
    std::set<std::string> uniqueNames(orderedNames.begin(), orderedNames.end());
    bool allUnique = (uniqueNames.size() == orderedNames.size());
    
    return OrderingSnapshot{orderedNames, allUnique};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<think>We are given the structure of the file and the function to implement.
 We need to use the reaction library to implement the function step by step.

 Steps:
 1. Create owner and field TrackingFieldNode instances.
 2. Get observer graph instance.

 4. Get field graph instance.
 5. Register field nodes under object ID in the field graph.

 8. Check if owner was notified before delete (by checking the owner's notification count).
 9. Clear owner notification count.
10. Clear dependencies on owner (so that the owner is no longer dependent on the field).
11. Remove field mappings for object ID (from the field graph).
12. Attempt to bind field again (should not be possible because the owner has been cleaned up?).
13. Trigger notification on field again.
14. Check if owner was notified after delete (should be false because the owner was cleaned up).
15. Close nodes (owner and field).
16. Return field graph cleanup result.

 We note that the function `test_field_graph_cleanup` takes an `objectId` as parameter.

 Let's break down the code accordingly.

 We assume that the `TrackingFieldNode` class is already defined as given.

 We'll use the following reaction library functions and classes:
   - `reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance()`
   - `reaction::ObserverGraph::addNode(ObserverNode&)`
   - `reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance()`
   - `reaction::FieldGraph::addObj(uint64_t, const NodePtr&)`
   - `reaction::FieldGraph::bindField(uint64_t, const NodePtr&, const NodePtr&)`
   - `reaction::ObserverNode::valueChanged(bool)` (which we override in TrackingFieldNode)
   - `reaction::ObserverNode::close()`

 We also have the `TrackingFieldNode` class that overrides `valueChanged` and has a `notifications` method.

 Implementation:

 We'll create two TrackingFieldNode instances: one for the owner and one for the field.

 Then we'll follow the steps.

 Note: The function returns a `FieldGraphCleanupResult` struct.

 Let's write the code accordingly.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(name) {}
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    int notifications() const { return m_notifications; }
    void clear() { m_notifications = 0; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    // 1. Create owner and field TrackingFieldNode instances
    TrackingFieldNode owner("owner");
    TrackingFieldNode field("field");
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Register nodes
    observerGraph.addNode(&owner);
    observerGraph.addNode(&field);
    
    // 4. Get field graph instance
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 5. Register field nodes under object ID
    fieldGraph.addObj(objectId, &field);
    
    // 6. Bind field to owner
    fieldGraph.bindField(objectId, &field, &owner);
    
    // 7. Trigger notification on field
    field.valueChanged();
    
    // 8. Check if owner was notified before delete
    bool notifiedBefore = owner.notifications() > 0;
    
    // 9. Clear owner notification count
    owner.clear();
    
    // 10. Clear dependencies on owner
    observerGraph.clearDependencies(&owner);
    
    // 11. Remove field mappings for object ID
    fieldGraph.removeObj(objectId);
    
    // 12. Attempt to bind field again
    // This should not create a new binding since the object ID was removed
    fieldGraph.bindField(objectId, &field, &owner);
    
    // 13. Trigger notification on field again
    field.valueChanged();
    
    // 14. Check if owner was notified after delete
    bool notifiedAfter = owner.notifications() > 0;
    
    // 15. Close nodes
    owner.close();
    field.close();
    
    // 16. Return field graph cleanup result
    return FieldGraphCleanupResult{notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AtomicCompoundSnapshot {
    int afterAddition;
    int afterMultiplication;
    int afterBitwiseAnd;
    int afterShift;
    int postIncrementReturn;
    int finalValue;
    int calcEvaluations;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate atomic compound assignments on a reactive counter.
 *
 * @return AtomicCompoundSnapshot Snapshot with values after each compound operation.
 */
inline AtomicCompoundSnapshot test_atomic_compound_assign() {
    // 1. Create reactive variable initialized to 1
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(1);
    
    // 2. Create counter to track calculation evaluations, starting at 0
    auto evaluationCounter = reaction::var(0);
    
    // 3. Create calculation to track value - increments counter when reactiveVar changes
    auto trackerCalc = reaction::calc([&]() {
        // Access reactiveVar to establish dependency
        reactiveVar.get();
        // Increment evaluation counter
        evaluationCounter += 1;
    });
    
    // Force initial evaluation to establish dependency graph
    trackerCalc.get();
    
    // Reset counter after initial setup
    evaluationCounter.value(0);
    
    // 4. Apply atomic addition: += 5
    reactiveVar += 5;
    // 5. Get value after addition
    int afterAddition = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // 6. Apply atomic multiplication: *= 3
    reactiveVar *= 3;
    // 7. Get value after multiplication
    int afterMultiplication = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // 8. Apply atomic bitwise AND: &= 0x0F
    reactiveVar &= 0x0F;
    // 9. Get value after bitwise AND
    int afterBitwiseAnd = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // 10. Apply atomic left shift: <<= 2
    reactiveVar <<= 2;
    // 11. Get value after shift
    int afterShift = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // 12. Apply post-increment: ++
    int postIncrementReturn = reactiveVar++;
    // 13. Get final value
    int finalValue = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // 14. Get number of evaluations from counter
    int calcEvaluations = evaluationCounter.get();
    
    return AtomicCompoundSnapshot{
        afterAddition,
        afterMultiplication,
        afterBitwiseAnd,
        afterShift,
        postIncrementReturn,
        finalValue,
        calcEvaluations
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct UnaryExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    bool logicalNotAfterFlip;
    int bitwiseNotValue;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate unary expression builders and operators on reactive values.
 *
 * This function showcases how low-level expression builder helpers (`make_binary_op`,
 * `make_unary_op`) and high-level unary operators (`-`, `!`, `~`) cooperate with the
 * `expr` factory to produce reactive expressions.
 *
 * @param a First input value participating in the sum expression
 * @param b Second input value participating in the sum expression
 * @return UnaryExpressionSnapshot Snapshot of expression outputs before/after updates
 */
inline UnaryExpressionSnapshot test_unary_expression_builders(int a, int b) {
    using namespace reaction;
    
    // 1. Create reactive variables
    auto var_a = var(a);
    auto var_b = var(b);
    
    // 2. Build negated sum expression: -(a + b)
    auto sum_expr = var_a + var_b;
    auto negated_sum_expr = -sum_expr;
    auto negated_sum_react = expr(negated_sum_expr);
    
    // 3. Create reactive boolean variable
    auto bool_var = var(true);
    
    // 4. Build logical NOT expression: !bool_var
    auto logical_not_expr = !bool_var;
    auto logical_not_react = expr(logical_not_expr);
    
    // 5. Create reactive integer variable
    auto int_var = var(0);
    
    // 6. Build bitwise NOT expression: ~int_var
    auto bitwise_not_expr = ~int_var;
    auto bitwise_not_react = expr(bitwise_not_expr);
    
    // 7. Get initial negated sum value
    int initial_negated_sum = negated_sum_react.get();
    
    // 8. Update variables to trigger recomputation
    var_a.value(10);
    var_b.value(20);
    bool_var.value(false);
    int_var.value(5);
    
    // 9. Get updated values
    int updated_negated_sum = negated_sum_react.get();
    bool logical_not_after_flip = logical_not_react.get();
    int bitwise_not_value = bitwise_not_react.get();
    
    // 10. Return unary expression snapshot
    return UnaryExpressionSnapshot{
        initial_negated_sum,
        updated_negated_sum,
        logical_not_after_flip,
        bitwise_not_value
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_thread_manager_force_register` function.

The function should:
1. Get thread manager instance
2. Reset thread manager state
3. Check thread safety before registration
4. Register main thread
5. Check thread safety after main thread registration
6. Create worker thread and register it
7. Wait for worker thread to complete
8. Check thread safety after worker thread registration
9. Return force register snapshot

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *
 * Collects observable state around calls to ThreadManager registration APIs.
 */
struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

/**
 * Function: test_thread_manager_force_register
 *
 * Demonstrates how registering multiple distinct threads causes
 * ThreadManager to enable thread safety.
 *
 * - We start from a clean ThreadManager state.
 * - The main thread registers itself and remains the sole thread.
 * - A worker thread then also registers, which should trigger
 *   multi-thread detection and enable thread safety.
 *
 * @return ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *         A snapshot of the thread-safety flag before any registration,
 *         after the main-thread registration, and after the worker-thread registration.
 */
inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register();

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_raw_expression_builders` function.

The function should:
1. Create reactive variables
2. Build sum expression
3. Build negated expression
4. Build chained expression
5. Get initial expression values
6. Update variables to trigger recomputation
7. Get updated expression values
8. Return raw expression snapshot

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: RawExpressionSnapshot
 *
 * Captures the values produced by raw expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) before and after updating
 * their reactive inputs.
 */
struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;

    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

/**
 * Function: test_raw_expression_builders
 *
 * Demonstrates how low-level expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) can be combined with
 * `expr` to build reactive calculations without using the higher
 * level `make_binary_op` / `make_unary_op` shortcuts.
 *
 * The function:
 * - Creates three reactive integers A, B, C.
 * - Builds sum = A + B via make_binary_expr.
 * - Builds negated = -(A + B) via make_unary_expr(make_binary_expr(...)).
 * - Builds chained = -(A + B) * C via nested make_* calls.
 * - Updates A and C and records all expression values before and after.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first input
 * @param b Initial value for the second input
 * @param c Initial value for the chaining multiplier
 * @return RawExpressionSnapshot
 *         Snapshot of sum / negated / chained values before and after updates.
 */
inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c);

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/expression/atomic_operations.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: AtomicHelperSnapshot
 *
 * Records the value of an integer after a sequence of atomic compound helper
 * operations and whether each operation reported a change.
 */
struct AtomicHelperSnapshot {
    int32_t finalValue;
    bool addReportedChange;
    bool multiplyReportedChange;
    bool andReportedChange;
    bool leftShiftReportedChange;
    bool incrementReportedChange;
};

/**
 * Helper implementation type that mimics the minimal interface required by
 * reaction::atomicCompoundAssign helpers. It stores an integer and exposes
 * an atomicOperation method that receives a lambda and tracks whether the
 * value was changed.
 */
class IntAtomicImpl {
public:
    explicit IntAtomicImpl(int32_t initial) : m_value(initial), m_lastReportedChange(false) {}

    template <typename F>
    void atomicOperation(F&& op) {
        bool changed = std::invoke(std::forward<F>(op), m_value);
        m_lastReportedChange = changed;
    }

    template <typename F>
    void atomicOperation(F&& op, bool forceNotify) {
        bool changed = std::invoke(std::forward<F>(op), m_value);
        if (forceNotify) {
            changed = true;
        }
        m_lastReportedChange = changed;
    }

    int32_t value() const { return m_value; }
    bool lastReportedChange() const { return m_lastReportedChange; }

private:
    int32_t m_value;
    bool m_lastReportedChange;
};

/**
 * Function: test_atomic_compound_helpers
 *
 * Demonstrates the low-level atomic compound helper APIs:
 * - atomicCompoundAssign
 * - atomicAddAssign
 * - atomicMultiplyAssign
 * - atomicBitwiseAndAssign
 * - atomicLeftShiftAssign
 * - atomicIncrement
 *
 * These helpers operate on a simple IntAtomicImpl type instead of a full
 * React wrapper, making it easy to observe value and change reporting.
 *
 * @param initial Initial integer value
 * @return AtomicHelperSnapshot
 *         Snapshot containing the final value and which operations reported
 *         that a change occurred.
 */
inline AtomicHelperSnapshot test_atomic_compound_helpers(int32_t initial) {
    IntAtomicImpl atomic(initial);
    
    // Reset change flags before starting
    atomic.atomicOperation([](int32_t&) { return false; });
    
    // Test atomic add assignment
    reaction::atomicAddAssign(atomic, 5);
    bool addChanged = atomic.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Test atomic multiply assignment  
    reaction::atomicMultiplyAssign(atomic, 3);
    bool multiplyChanged = atomic.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Test atomic bitwise AND assignment
    reaction::atomicBitwiseAndAssign(atomic, 0x0F);
    bool andChanged = atomic.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Test atomic left shift assignment
    reaction::atomicLeftShiftAssign(atomic, 2);
    bool leftShiftChanged = atomic.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Test atomic increment
    reaction::atomicIncrement(atomic);
    bool incrementChanged = atomic.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Get final value
    int32_t finalVal = atomic.value();
    
    return AtomicHelperSnapshot{
        finalVal,
        addChanged,
        multiplyChanged,
        andChanged,
        leftShiftChanged,
        incrementChanged
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

This implementation:

1. **IntAtomicImpl class**: Implements the required interface with atomic operation tracking
2. **atomicOperation methods**: Properly handle change detection and notification
3. **test_atomic_compound_helpers function**: 
   - Uses all specified reaction library atomic compound assignment functions
   - Applies operations in the specified sequence
   - Checks change reporting after each operation
   - Returns a complete AtomicHelperSnapshot with all required fields
4. **Proper includes and namespace**: Maintains the given structure and dependencies

The implementation follows the exact specifications and uses the reaction library functions as documented in the reference materials.</answer>
